sn15.19:1.1: Near Sāvatthī. There the Blessed One said:
sn15.19:1.2: “From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on.
sn15.19:1.3: A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.19:1.4: A being who has not been your daughter at one time in the past is not easy to find.…
sn15.19:1.6: From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on.
sn15.19:1.7: A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.19:1.8: Long have you thus experienced stress, experienced pain, experienced loss, swelling the cemeteries—
sn15.19:1.9: enough to become disenchanted with all fabricated things, enough to become dispassionate, enough to be released.”
